heading: Our Story
paragraph1: In 1989, Mrs Janine Grant and a group of volunteers from the North of Mauritius noticed that many children in Trou-aux-Biches were either not in school or severely behind academically. They opened a small reading workshop in the courtyard of the Notre Dame de Fatima parish, with just 14 students.
paragraph2: Today, the association welcomes over 130 students each year — young people aged 11 to 18 from disadvantaged backgrounds, guided by 15 teachers. Under the presidency of Pauline Martin and the direction of Maryline Lamy, more than 2,000 students have found their way back to confidence and success.
paragraph3: Our goal: to help them progress, flourish and become the citizens of tomorrow in a welcoming environment, adapted to their needs, pace and reality.
quote: Through support based on love, respect and the appreciation of each individual, more than 2,000 students have found their way back to confidence, self-esteem and success.
mainImageAlt: The school building in the courtyard of the Fatima parish
